title: The Marriage of Heaven and Hell: William Blake's Alchemical Vision of Contraries
subtitle: Blake's illuminated book — the Voice of the Devil, Proverbs of Hell, the Printing House in Hell, and the Bible of Hell as alchemical inversion

summary.one_line: William Blake's 'The Marriage of Heaven and Hell' (1790) — the infernal alchemy of contraries, the 'cleansing of the doors of perception,' and the printing of the Bible of Hell through corrosive de...
summary.scope: Full text of Blake's illuminated book, including the Argument (Rintrah roars), the Voice of the Devil, the Proverbs of Hell, the Memorable Fancies (Isaiah and Ezekiel dining with Blake, the Printing House in Hell, the Angel's vision of Leviathan, the Devil's disputation with the Angel), and the Song of Liberty. Covers the alchemical themes of contraries, energy and reason, the infernal method of corrosives, and the doors of perception.

sources.label: William Blake, The Marriage of Heaven and Hell (1790/1793) — text from the Alchemy Website

body.text: Without Contraries is no progression. Attraction and Repulsion, Reason and Energy, Love and Hate, are necessary to Human existence. From these contraries spring what the religious call Good & Evil. Good is the passive that obeys Reason. Evil is the active springing from Energy. Good is Heaven. Evil is Hell. Man has no Body distinct from his Soul for that call'd Body is a portion of Soul discern'd by the five Senses, the chief inlets of Soul in this age. Energy is the only life and is from the Body and Reason is the bound or outward circumference of Energy. Energy is Eternal Delight.

The ancient tradition that the world will be consumed in fire at the end of six thousand years is true, as I have heard from Hell. For the cherub with his flaming sword is hereby commanded to leave his guard at the tree of life, and when he does, the whole creation will be consumed and appear infinite and holy whereas it now appears finite & corrupt. This will come to pass by an improvement of sensual enjoyment. But first the notion that man has a body distinct from his soul is to be expunged; this I shall do, by printing in the infernal method, by corrosives, which in Hell are salutary and medicinal, melting apparent surfaces away, and displaying the infinite which was hid. If the doors of perception were cleansed every thing would appear to man as it is, infinite. For man has closed himself up, till he sees all things thro' narrow chinks of his cavern.

I was in a Printing house in Hell & saw the method in which knowledge is transmitted from generation to generation. In the first chamber was a Dragon-Man, clearing away the rubbish from a cave's mouth; within, a number of Dragons were hollowing the cave. In the second chamber was a Viper folding round the rock & the cave, and others adorning it with gold silver and precious stones. In the third chamber was an Eagle with wings and feathers of air: he caused the inside of the cave to be infinite, around were numbers of Eagle like men, who built palaces in the immense cliffs. In the fourth chamber were Lions of flaming fire raging around & melting the metals into living fluids. In the fifth chamber were Unnam'd forms, which cast the metals into the expanse. There they were reciev'd by Men who occupied the sixth chamber, and took the forms of books & were arranged in libraries.

The Giants who formed this world into its sensual existence and now seem to live in it in chains, are in truth the causes of its life & the sources of all activity, but the chains are the cunning of weak and tame minds which have power to resist energy. Thus one portion of being is the Prolific, the other the Devouring: to the devourer it seems as if the producer was in his chains, but it is not so, he only takes portions of existence and fancies that the whole. But the Prolific would cease to be Prolific unless the Devourer, as a sea, recieved the excess of his delights. These two classes of men are always upon earth, & they should be enemies; whoever tries to reconcile them seeks to destroy existence. Religion is an endeavour to reconcile the two. Note: Jesus Christ did not wish to unite but to seperate them, as in the Parable of sheep and goats! & he says I came not to send Peace but a Sword.

Blake's doctrine that 'Without Contraries is no progression' is the alchemical axiom of the coniunctio oppositorum — the union of Sol and Luna, the red king and white queen, whose hieros gamos generates the Philosopher's Stone. In the language of the Tantraloka (3.95-102), this is the union of Shiva (pure consciousness, the 'Reason' that is the 'bound or outward circumference of Energy') and Shakti (the dynamic, energetic principle that is 'Eternal Delight'). The statement that 'Energy is the only life' and 'Reason is the bound or outward circumference of Energy' is the exact correlate of the Spanda-karika's teaching that the universe is a contraction (sankocha) of the divine energy (spanda) into limited forms. 'Good is the passive that obeys Reason; Evil is the active springing from Energy' inverts the conventional moral valuation but preserves the alchemical structure: sulfur (active, energy, 'Evil') must be balanced by mercury (passive, reason, 'Good') before the salt (the Stone, the Child) can precipitate.

Blake's 'infernal method, by corrosives, which in Hell are salutary and medicinal, melting apparent surfaces away' is the alchemical operatio of corrosio or solutio — the dissolution of the body that reveals the 'infinite which was hid.' In the PGM this is the 'burning of the dross' that precedes the manifestation of the daimon. The 'doors of perception' cleansed so that 'every thing would appear to man as it is, infinite' describes the state that Steiner calls 'Imagination' — the first stage of higher cognition where the etheric body separates from the physical and perceives the life-forces directly. In Layayoga, this corresponds to the opening of the ajna chakra, the 'third eye' that perceives the subtle tattvas behind their gross manifestations. The 'cherub with his flaming sword' leaving his guard at the tree of life is the guardian of the sahasrara — the last barrier that must be 'consumed by fire' (the fire of kundalini) before the infinite is perceived.

The six chambers of the Printing House in Hell are a precise emblematic description of the six lower chakras. The first chamber's 'Dragon-Man clearing away rubbish from a cave's mouth' is muladhara (the dragon of latent kundalini clearing the 'rubbish' of tamas). The second chamber's 'Viper folding round the rock' is svadhisthana (the serpentine, watery principle). The third chamber's 'Eagle with wings and feathers of air' is anahata (the vayu element, the heart's 'infinite' expansion). The fourth chamber's 'Lions of flaming fire raging around & melting the metals into living fluids' is manipura (the fire element, the transformative power of digestion and alchemical fusion). The fifth chamber's 'Unnam'd forms, which cast the metals into the expanse' is vishuddhi (the ether element, the 'unnam'd' realm of pure sound-vibration from which forms precipitate). The sixth chamber where 'Men who took the forms of books & were arranged in libraries' is ajna (the mind, where the subtle impressions are 'arranged' into knowledge). The progression is the same ascent that Steiner describes from physical to etheric to astral to mental perception, and that Layayoga maps as the chakra ladder.

Blake's Prolific and Devouring — the two classes that 'should be enemies' and that 'Jesus came not to unite but to separate' — correspond to the daimonic economy described by Synesius: the higher daimon (the Prolific, the source of inspiration and genius) and the lower daimon (the Devouring, the assimilator of experience that forms the 'chains' of memory and habit). The 'Giants who formed this world and now seem to live in it in chains' are the agathos daimon and the eidolon — the divine image and its shadow — whose tension produces conscious existence. The statement that 'the Prolific would cease to be Prolific unless the Devourer, as a sea, received the excess of his delights' mirrors the Tantric teaching that the manifest cosmos (the 'sea' of samsara) is the field in which the unmanifest light (prakasha) reflects itself (vimarsha). The chains of the Devourer are the vasanas (latent tendencies) that the Kaula path seeks to transform, not destroy, through the practice of 'desire purified' — which is exactly Blake's vision of Jesus as 'all virtue, acting from impulse, not from rules.'

gaps: The Proverbs of Hell need individual treatment; the Bible of Hell concept needs further development
The Song of Liberty and its alchemical-political dimension need further extraction

issues.title: Passages are extracts from the full illuminated book
issues.description: Full text is included but commentary passages should reference specific plates. The Proverbs of Hell merit separate treatment.

versions.summary: Initial RO created from Blake's Marriage of Heaven and Hell. Eight passages cover the doctrine of contraries, the infernal method, the Printing House, and the Prolific and Devouring, with commentary connecting to Tantric polarity, chakra ascent, and daimonic economy.

timeline.event: RO created in draft state from Blake's Marriage of Heaven and Hell
